summary: @wagni_bot/polymarket-sdk postinstall harvests host identity and filesystem contents
details: The package ships a postinstall.js that runs automatically on `npm install`. The script imports `fs`, `os`, and `http`, then reads host identity (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.homedir()) and probes filesystem paths beneath the user's home directory via repeated fs.existsSync() calls before sending data out over HTTP. This pattern — install-time collection of system identity and home-directory contents combined with a network channel — matches credential/filesystem exfiltration rather than any legitimate SDK install task, and the package does not document any such behavior. Installing this package causes the installer's machine to leak host and filesystem data to an author-controlled endpoint at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
